_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
procedure_name: Follow up cases of Molar pregnancy requiring chemotherapy
source_files: Follow-up case of Molar pregnancy requiring Chemotherapy_Medco.pdf
Follow-up case of Molar pregnancy requiring Chemotherapy_PPD.CPD.pdf
clinical_indications: Patient presents with persistent Vaginal bleeding and elevation of serum beta-hCG levels after uterine evacuation
History of the following in preceding pregnancy/h/o uterine evacuation of a diagnosed mole/evacuation for missed abortion/other type of pregnancy termination:
Abnormal vaginal bleeding generally in early pregnancy but in exceptional cases diagnosis could be delayed to later in pregnancy.
Uterus size large for gestational age
Pain from large benign theca-lutein cysts
Pelvic/Abdominal Ultrasound scan showing a vascular or honeycomb appearance / snowstorm appearance of mixed echogenicity & ovaries containing multiple large theca-lutein cysts
High levels of serum beta-hCG (often exceeding 100,000 IU/L)

preauth.label: Detailed Clinical notes (including history, symptoms, signs, physical examination, B-HCG levels trend, history of molar pregnancy, indication for procedure and advice for admission, and serial number of cycle being administered)
USG abdomen (essential for first chemotherapy cycle)

claim.label: Detailed indoor case papers (clearly indicating the chemotherapy drug administered)

ppd_preauth.q: Are detailed Clinical notes all vitals, detailed history, symptoms, signs, physical examination, investigation report B-HCG levels, trend of serum B-HCG levels must be documented, indication for procedure and advice for admission available?
Does the clinical note contain serial number of cycle being administered?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed Indoor Case Papers available clearly indicating the chemotherapy drug administered?
Is discharge summary available with follow-up advice at the time of discharge?

common_queries: Patient history of molar pregnancy not documented or documented as 'No'.
Clinical presentation, physical examination, or supporting investigations (high beta-hCG levels) not indicative of the procedure.
Trend of beta-hCG levels not documented.
Serial number of chemotherapy cycle not mentioned in clinical notes.
Indoor case papers do not clearly indicate the chemotherapy drug administered.

clinical_key_pointers: Gestational trophoblastic disease (GTD) is a tumor originating from the trophoblast (abnormal placenta), which surrounds the blastocyst and develops into the chorion and amnion. It is composed of a spectrum of premalignant to malignant disorders. The most common form of GTD is hydatidiform mole, also known as molar pregnancy.
Follow-up is done to confirm the success of treatment given and to identify women with persistent or malignant GTD, such patients may require adjuvant chemotherapy or rarely surgery.
The risk of persistent or recurrent GTD is greatest in the first 12 months after evacuation especially within the first 6 months.

A number of chemotherapy regimens are used for treating the disease, like methotrexate, actinomycin D. The commonest regime is methotrexate with leucovorin. Alternative regimes may use Actinomycin D or multidrug therapy.
The indications mentioned are more pronounced in complete molar pregnancy and less in partial mole.
additional_information.package_usage_notes: Repeat booking for the same package will be made. The number of times patient will require hospitalization will depend on the trend of Beta-HCG levels. Number of cycles being booked should be clearly mentioned.
